items.title: Support Vector Machines
items.description: SVM dual optimization depends on dot products between training points; kernels plug into SVM as the primary nonlinear upgrade path.

items.title: Matrix multiplication and inner products
items.description: K(x, x′) generalizes the dot product x · x′; Gram matrices are built from pairwise inner products in feature space.

items.title: Supervised learning paradigm
items.description: Kernel SVMs are trained on labeled (x, y) pairs like other supervised classifiers; kernel choice shapes the decision function f(x).

items.title: Deep learning and learned representations
items.description: Neural networks learn φ from data instead of fixing K by hand; both approaches reshape geometry so simple decision rules work in a richer space.

items.title: Distance metrics
items.description: RBF kernels transform squared Euclidean distance ‖x−x′‖² into similarity; metric and scaling choices affect kernel geometry.
